model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Close the multiple-settlement error proof gap without changing the SDK-native deactivation placement or lifecycle contract.
source_of_truth: workers/dkr-1-cleanup-v3/cleanup-contract.json, its saved executable probe and replay, the inherited v2 contract and checkpoint, the prior validator record, and current SDK and Lite source
read_method: Run workers/dkr-1-cleanup-v3/replay.sh and compare the inherited 10 cases, 6 activation behaviors, new multiple-error case, checkpoint shape, wall gate, and all eight evidence hashes.
questions_answered: Two rejected settlement promises plus one still-pending normal settlement keep deactivation pending until the normal attempt settles.
All snapshotted controllers abort before any settlement is delivered.
AggregateError.errors contains the original failures in deterministic snapshot order even when rejection order is reversed.
The added case makes zero commits and leaves durable status and version unchanged.
The inherited replay preserves the prior ten cleanup cases, one-error propagation, normal cancelled settlement, six activation behaviors, race ordering, idempotency, and SDK-only placement.
questions_unanswered: A separate validator has not replayed checkpoint v3 or challenged its new aggregation case.
The current product still uses shutdown to begin finish on ordinary cleanup, so cleanup_business_state_mutation_count remains 1 until a separately authorized resolution and progression change.
Parent-child structured cancellation and provider-specific descendant joining remain assigned outside this cleanup revision.
decision: candidate only; never accepted by this worker

wall_gate.reason: The current product cleanup wall remains breached and checkpoint v3 lacks independent replay.

confidence_probability_update: 0.92 after v2 to 0.96 after the deterministic three-attempt, two-failure aggregation case
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The new case closes the prior non-replayable AggregateError branch without changing placement or lifecycle semantics.
Reverse rejection order proves AggregateError.errors follows the snapshotted attempt order rather than completion order.
Keeping a normal attempt pending proves deactivation does not reject before every snapshotted settlement completes.
The current product wall remains 1 against 0 and blocks progression independently of this discovery result.
No Lite primitive or product change is supported by this revision.

candidate_ckrs.target: 11/11 cleanup cases while cleanup_business_state_mutation_count remains 0 after implementation

candidate_pkrs.scope: Replace shutdown with deactivation and enforce the eleven scope-seam cases only after independent checkpoint acceptance and a recorded wall resolution.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
